creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text:
I discovered that "setfib 1 netperf -t UDP_STREAM" would timeout.  On further inspection, I found that source address selection for UDP packets with SO_DONTROUTE set was always using the default FIB.  It should be using the process FIB instead.

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: Configure two interfaces on different subnets with different fibs.  Try running netperf -t UDP_STREAM on a nondefault FIB.  I also have an ATF test case for this bug, but it requires a special C helper program to set SO_DONTROUTE.  I will post it as soon as I can.
